manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: asp, radish, chemistry, apple, mesa, garlic, hallway, anatomy, teacher.
Your opponent's team words are: rattlesnake, geology, glacier, mustard, copperhead, potato, window, cashier.
Distractor words are: turnip, parsley, foundation, valley, green, evergreen, doctor.
The kill words are: door.

message.content:
.CLUE: SCHOOL
TARGETS: teacher, hallway, chemistry, anatomy
message.reasoning:
We need to provide a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words, not opponent, distractor, kill. Must avoid any of those words. Target words must be from our list. Provide clue and list.

Our team words: asp, radish, chemistry, apple, mesa (a table? also a flat-topped hill), garlic, hallway, anatomy, teacher.

We can choose multiple related words. Need to ensure clue not one of any listed words or containing parts. Also ensure clue not ambiguous leading partner to guess opponent/distractor. Provide best clue.

Think of a concept linking e.g., "biology" related to anatomy, teacher (maybe?), chemistry? Could be "science". Science relates to chemistry, anatomy, maybe teacher (science teacher) but not asp, radish, apple, etc. Could also be "food" linking radish, apple, garlic. Could be "plant". Plant relates to radish, apple, garlic. Not the others though.

Maybe we want to clue multiple words: "biology" could link anatomy, teacher (biology teacher), chemistry maybe, but not others. "Garden" could link radish, garlic, apple? Apple is fruit tree.

What about "classroom"? hallway, teacher, maybe anatomy (class?), chemistry (lab). Could be "school". School relates to teacher, hallway, chemistry (class), maybe anatomy (class), but not the produce.

We want to maximize number of related target words while being safe. Could do "science" for chemistry, anatomy, teacher (science teacher). But radish, apple, garlic, mesa could be under "nature". "Nature" would relate to radish, apple, garlic, mesa, asp (snake). That's many.

But need to avoid linking to opponent/distractor. "Nature" might also link to "green" distractor, "valley", "evergreen", "turnip", "parsley". That's okay, but partner might guess those. But we list specific targets. The clue can be ambiguous but we give list.

Could we propose "botany" linking radish, apple, garlic, maybe mesa? Not really. Not teacher. "Food" linking radish, apple, garlic. That's three.

Maybe "lesson" linking teacher, hallway, chemistry class, anatomy class. Could be.

We can provide clue that relates to many: "lab" relates to chemistry, anatomy (lab), maybe teacher, hallway (lab hallway). But radish, apple not.

Better: "fruit" links apple, radish? radish not fruit. So not.

Maybe "diet" links radish, apple, garlic. But also could link to "turnip" (distractor). That risk.

Let's decide to target 4 words: radish, apple, garlic, mesa? Not.

Maybe "school" gives teacher, hallway, chemistry, anatomy. That's four. No risk of opponent/distractor. "School" is not any of the listed. It might also hint at "turnip"? Not.

Ok clue: SCHOOL. Targets: teacher, hallway, chemistry, anatomy.

Check: Are any of those opponent words associated with school? "cashier"? maybe not. "window"? could be school window but unlikely. It's okay.

Thus format:

CLUE: SCHOOL
TARGETS: teacher, hallway, chemistry, anatomy

Make sure no extra text
